'''Jackson Hastings''' (born 4 January 1996) is a [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]], [[Sydney Roosters]] and the [[Wests Tigers]] in the NRL, and the [[Salford Red Devils]] and the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]] and finally [[Newcastle Knights]].

On 6 October 2019, he was crowned [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]].

==Background==
Hastings was born in [[Wollongong|Wollongong, New South Wales]], [[Australia]], and is of [[British people|English]] descent through his grandmother.<ref>{{cite web|date=27 September 2018|title=Salford coach Ian Watson tips Jackson Hastings for England|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11510788/salford-coach-ian-watson-tips-jackson-hastings-for-england|publisher=Sky Sports|access-date=28 September 2018}}</ref>

He played his junior football for the [[Western Suburbs Red Devils]] before being signed by the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]].{{cn|date=July 2025}}

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2012 and 2013, Hastings played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] Under 16s and Under 18s teams respectively.<ref name="foxsportspulse">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsportspulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2168-0-0-0&sID=227522|title=NSW Under 16s - NSWRL Academy - FOX SPORTS PULSE|publisher=foxsportspulse.com|access-date=2014-09-04}}</ref><ref name="dragons">{{cite web|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/news/2013/06/17/nyc-quartet-selected-for-nsw-u18-origin.html|title=NYC Quartet Selected for NSW U18 Origin - Dragons|publisher=dragons.com.au|access-date=2014-09-04|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140904205111/http://www.dragons.com.au/news/2013/06/17/nyc-quartet-selected-for-nsw-u18-origin.html|archive-date=4 September 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 2013, Hastings played for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team,<ref name="dailytelegraph">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/sydney-roosters-ready-to-join-fight-to-sign-son-of-legendary-kevin-horrie-hastings/story-e6frexr9-1226637068567|title=Sydney Roosters ready to join fight to sign son of legendary Kevin 'Horrie' Hastings|publisher=dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2014-09-04}}</ref> and was selected for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]].

Hastings played 47 games, scored 14 tries, kicked 195 goals and 1 field goal for 447 points in his U20s career from 2013 to 2016 with both the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://18thman.com/national-youth-competition/players/h/|title=18thman - U20s Player Records|website=18thman.com}}</ref>

===Sydney Roosters===
On 27 August 2013, Hastings signed with the [[Sydney Roosters]] on a 3-year contract,<ref name="26rounds">{{cite web|url=http://26rounds.com/2013/08/27/roosters-officially-sign-jackson-hastings-to-a-three-year-deal-from-2014/|title=Roosters officially sign Jackson Hastings to a three year deal from 2014 | 26 Rounds|publisher=26rounds.com|access-date=2014-09-04|archive-date=4 September 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140904203416/http://26rounds.com/2013/08/27/roosters-officially-sign-jackson-hastings-to-a-three-year-deal-from-2014/|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="zerotackle">{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/jackson-hastings-signs-with-roosters-10908/|title=Jackson Hastings signs with Roosters | NRL | Zero Tackle|publisher=zerotackle.com|access-date=2014-09-04|archive-date=8 September 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140908023929/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/jackson-hastings-signs-with-roosters-10908/|url-status=dead}}</ref> rejecting offers from St. George Illawarra, [[North Queensland Cowboys]] and [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref name="dailytelegraph2">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/wayne-bennett-nick-politis-go-after-horries-son-jackson-hastings/story-fni3gn3s-1226647232674|title=Wayne Bennett, Nick Politis go after Horrie's son, Jackson Hastings|publisher=dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2014-09-04}}</ref> He played for the Roosters' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2014.

In round 26 of the [[2014 NRL season]], Hastings made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the [[Sydney Roosters]] against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]. The following week, Hastings played in the club's upset qualifying loss to Penrith.<ref name="roosters">{{cite web|url=http://www.roosters.com.au/news/2014/09/04/late_mail_robbo_names_debutant.html|title=Late Mail: Robbo names debutant - Roosters|publisher=roosters.com.au|access-date=2014-09-04}}</ref><ref name="dailytelegraph3">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/the-18yearold-son-of-roosters-great-kevin-hastings-jackson-will-make-his-debut-against-south-sydney-at-allianz-stadium/story-fnp0lyn3-1227046909807|title=The 18-year-old son of Roosters great Kevin Hastings, Jackson, will make his debut against South Sydney at Allianz Stadium|publisher=dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2014-09-04}}</ref><ref name="zerotackle2">{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/jackson-hastings-to-make-his-debut-14781/|title=Jackson Hastings to make his debut | NRL | Zero Tackle|publisher=zerotackle.com|access-date=2014-09-04|archive-date=4 September 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140904194437/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/jackson-hastings-to-make-his-debut-14781/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
[[File:JACKSON HASTINGS.jpg|thumb|right|Hastings playing for the [[Newtown Jets]] in 2014]]
On 2 May 2015, Hastings played for the [[Junior Kangaroos]] against [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx|title=Representative Round team lists|publisher=NRL.com|date=2015-04-28|access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref> 
On 8 July 2015, Hastings played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] Under-20s team against the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] Under-20s team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/07/04/pay_s_say_nsw_under_.html|title=PAY'S SAY: NSW Under-20s side|publisher=NSWRL.com.au|access-date=2015-07-09}}</ref>
In 2015, Hastings was a regular in the Sydney Roosters team as they won the Minor Premiership. Hastings played in the club's preliminary final loss to Brisbane. In 2016, Hastings had an indifferent year at the club as they struggled on the field. This culminated as Hastings was demoted to reserve grade with the [[Wyong Roos]] after he had disciplinary problems and also falling out with senior players at the club. In November 2016, Hastings was released from the final year of his contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/jackson-hastings-decsribes-sydney-roosters-exit-as-rubbish/aa06a098-a616-4f92-9d54-725e060136bb/|title=Hastings says Roosters exit was "rubbish"|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

===Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles===
In December 2016, Hastings signed a two-year contract with the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] starting in 2017.<ref>{{cite web|author=SeaEagles.com.au|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/manly-sign-jackson-hastings-24806/|title=Manly sign Jackson Hastings|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2016-04-12|access-date=2016-12-23}}</ref>
Hastings made his Debut for the  [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]]  in Round 3 of the [[2017 NRL season]]   against the  [[North Queensland Cowboys]] coming off the bench and filling  in at Fullback and Scoring a try as the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] defeated  the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]  30-8.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/35884|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=2017-08-18|title=NRL 2017 - Round 3}}</ref>

At the start of the 2018 season, Hastings was involved in a well-publicised falling-out with fellow Manly player [[Daly Cherry-Evans]] and coach [[Trent Barrett]].  He was banished to reserve grade to play for Blacktown Workers and told he would be moved on by the club.<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/lussick-let-rip-at-barrett-before-taking-toronto-lifeline-20180421-p4zaxr.html| title = Lussick let rip at Barrett before taking Toronto lifeline}}</ref><ref>{{cite web| url = http://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/jackson-hastings-manly-sea-eagles-nrl-trent-barrett-daly-cherry-evans-lyall-gorman/1we0jniss2ee19lowniey19zl| title = Manly Sea Eagles to name Jackson Hastings following well-publicised axing {{!}} Sportingnews}}</ref>
[[File:Jackson Hastings.jpg|thumb|right|350px|Hastings playing for the [[Blacktown Workers]] in 2018]]
On 26 June 2018, Hastings was released from the remainder of his contract with Manly.  The club released a statement saying "Manly Warringah Sea Eagles have today released Jackson Hastings from the remainder of his contract, effective immediately, The Club and Jackson's management have made the decision mutually".<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/jackson-hastings-released-from-manly/news-story/28dcac5b9f56df1a9ffd45648808baf5| title = Jackson Hastings released from Manly Sea Eagles effective immediately {{!}} news.com.au}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 12 July 2018, Hastings signed a two-year deal to join British side the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44807351| title = Jackson Hastings: Salford Red Devils sign former NRL half-back - BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web| url = http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12207/11434173/salford-sign-australian-jackson-hastings-to-boost-super-league-fight| title = Salford sign Australian Jackson Hastings to boost Super League fight {{!}} Rugby League News {{!}} Sky Sports}}</ref>

On 8 July 2019, Hastings was announced to have signed a 2-year deal with Super League side [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], effective from the start of the 2020 season.

On 6 October 2019, Hastings won the [[Man of Steel award]] after being voted as the [[Super League]]'s best player during the [[Super League XXIV]] season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/breaking-jackson-hastings-wins-super-20526567/|title=Jackson Hastings wins Super League Man of Steel after leading Salford to Grand Final|website=Mirror.co.uk|access-date=15 October 2019|archive-date=7 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191007131622/https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/breaking-jackson-hastings-wins-super-20526567|url-status=dead}}</ref>
Hastings was part of the Salford side which reached the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] but were defeated 23-6 by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].  Salford were looking to win their first championship since 1976.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian}}</ref><ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
Hastings joined [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] ahead of the [[2020 Super League season]].

Hastings played in the [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] which Wigan lost 8-4 against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

On 19 April 2021 Hastings signed a two-year deal to join [[NRL]] side the [[Wests Tigers]] starting in 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-contracts-full-squads-will-chambers-sharks-transfer-centre-signings-every-team/news-story/a9b3ee78e067f66b0e163be2259829f5/|title=Tigers confirm Hastings capture as Chambers signs on with Sharks: Transfer Centre|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

In round 7 of the [[2021 Super League season]] against Salford, Hastings kicked a field goal to win the match for Wigan 17-16.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-16-17-wigan-jackson-hastings-drop-goal-secures-win-against-former-club/|title=Jackson Hastings drop goal secures win against former club|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===Wests Tigers===
Wests Tigers signed Hastings on a two-year deal worth about $500,000 a season with a possible upgrade next year. In round 1 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Hastings made his club debut for the [[Wests Tigers]] in their 26-16 loss against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-03-12/nrl-roosters-knights-warriors-dragons-tigers-storm/100891316/|title=Melbourne Storm fight back to down Wests Tigers 26-16 after Newcastle, St George Illawarra record NRL wins|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref>

In round 6, Hastings helped the Wests Tigers to their first win of the 2022 season, kicking a 38 metre field goal to break a 20-all deadlock against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] with seconds left remaining in the match.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/tigers-hero-jackson-hastings-mic-drop-says-it-all-about-luke-brooks/news-story/cd1ef6eb7be246400fbdf4c57257cc8d/|title=Tigers hero Jackson Hastings mic drop says it all about Luke Brooks|website=www.news.com.au}}</ref>
On 31 July 2022, Hastings was ruled out for the remainder of the [[2022 NRL season]] after suffering a broken ankle in Wests Tigers upset victory over [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/2022-broncos-v-wests-tigers-patrick-carrigan-hip-drop-tackle-jackson-hastings-injury-video/d31043e3-dbfe-45cc-b5f5-33d20b8fadc8/|title=Coach blasts 'horrendous' tackle that cruelled Wests Tigers' Jackson Hastings|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref> Despite his 16 appearances, he was still second in the NRL in the "line engaged" stat.<ref>{{cite web| website= nrl.com| title=Line Engaged| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220906073511/https://www.nrl.com/stats/players/?competition=111&season=2022&stat=1000025|access-date=6 September 2022|url=https://www.nrl.com/stats/players/?competition=111&season=2022&stat=1000025|archive-date=6 September 2022}}</ref>
The Wests Tigers club would finish the [[2022 NRL season]] on the bottom of the table and claim the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]] for the first time.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-09-04/nrl-knights-sharks-tigers-raiders/101402294/|title=Canberra Raiders hand out 56-10 NRL drubbing to Wests Tigers as Cronulla beats Newcastle Knights 38-16|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref>

===Newcastle Knights===
In November 2022, Hastings was released from the final year of his Wests Tigers contract and signed a three-year deal with the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-11-16 |title=Knights sign Jackson Hastings |url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2022/11/16/knights-sign-jackson-hastings/ |access-date=2022-11-16 |website=Newcastle Knights |language=en}}</ref>

In round 1 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Hastings made his club debut for Newcastle in their 20–12 loss against the [[New Zealand Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-live-newcastle-knights-vs-new-zealand-warriors-live-scores-highlights-supercoach-live-team-lists-kalyn-ponga-warriors-recruits-signings/news-story/c97130d1157437d83818cdc1d7d75a4e/|title=‘Back on home soil’: CNK's ‘outstanding’ return, Knights ‘massive’ Ponga blow — Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>
In round 18 of the 2023 NRL season, Hastings scored two tries in Newcastle's 66-0 victory over Canterbury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-canterbury-bulldogs-v-newcastle-knights-live-blog-videos-stats-teams-replays-ins-and-outs-khaled-rajab-karl-oloapu-dane-gagai/news-story/542d580e73950b6aeb8b550cca212660/|title=‘This is f***ing disgusting’: Embarrassing Dogs hit for 66 amid Best hat-trick - Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>
Hastings made 22 appearances for Newcastle in the 2023 NRL season as the club finished 5th on the table. Hastings played in the club's elimination final victory over Canberra but missed the following week against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in which Newcastle's season was ended.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-newcastle-knights-season-review/eb7fed33aeb7f61ca8030120#:~:text=The%20Knights'%20season%20was%20languishing,rugby%20league%20fans%20in%20general./|title=NRL 2023: Newcastle Knights season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

After Newcastle lost the opening two matches of the [[2024 NRL season]], Hastings was demoted to reserve grade and replaced with [[Jack Cogger]]. It was reported that Hastings refused to train with the reserve grade team after being demoted.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-jackson-hastings-refuses-to-train-with-nsw-cup-newcastle-knights-dropped-why-adam-obrien-jack-cogger-tyson-gamble-nrl-360/news-story/5ffc8831ec810b3ae105c7a5bd6c61c0/|title=Inside brutal Hastings call... and how the Knights halfback responded to O’Brien question|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>
On 1 April 2024, Fox Sports commentator [[Andrew Voss]] was forced to apologise over a comment he made about Hastings during Newcastle's round 4 match against the New Zealand Warriors. The camera was panning to some of the NSW Cup players from Newcastle who were watching the first grade team play when Voss commented that one of the players was Hastings who appeared to be sleeping on the hill. Hastings took to his twitter account posting "Unsure who is commenting our game but to make a false statement about me sleeping on the hill when I am in the grandstand watching the game is ridiculous but not surprising to go with multiple lies told over the last few weeks". Voss then replied to Hastings saying “Apology to you Jackson. Certainly wasn’t delivered with any critical intent. Mistake made, Hope we are calling you in the top grade against next week v Dragons".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/commentator-apologises-after-jackson-hastings-fumes-at-remark/news-story/1d93cc6f5aea0cab19039dce7dce7858/|title=Commentator apologises after Jackson Hastings fumes at remark|website=www.news.com.au}}</ref>
Hastings played 16 matches for Newcastle in the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club finished 8th and qualified for the finals. They were eliminated in the first week of the finals by North Queensland.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-newcastle-knights-season-review-adam-obrien-20240917-p5kb9j.html/|title=Brutal reality Newcastle must accept before solving confidence-killing 'riddle'|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

Hastings started the [[2025 NRL season]] with Newcastle's reserve grade side after being frozen out of the first team squad by head coach Adam O'Brien. After spending the first 14 weeks in reserve grade, Hastings was called into the Newcastle side for their round 15 match against the Sydney Roosters.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.msn.com/en-au/sport/rugby_league/not-obsessed-with-pay-packets-adam-o-brien-explains-jackson-hastings-knights-exile/ar-AA1CVJdK?cvid=6bf5a92ef0844c67805856393c17228e&ocid=xmmo/|title=‘Not obsessed with pay packets': Adam O'Brien explains Jackson Hastings' Knights exile|website=www.msn.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-round-14-teams-ins-and-outs-jackson-hastings-to-return-for-knights-origin-selections-dragons-replacements-cody-walker-injury-tom-trbojevic-return/news-story/95639eeed56ce83f5d28c8dcb89eccf5/|title=Path cleared for $800k star’s return; Flanno’s big headache: Teams Chat LIVE|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>
Hastings managed to only make five appearances for Newcastle in the 2025 NRL season, which saw the club finish last and claim the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-newcastle-knights-press-conference-adam-obrien-final-game-reaction-6610-wooden-spoon-rugby-league-news/news-story/0d2382b73d882446b5f5f57abf1a740b/|title=‘Bulls**t’: Dejected O’Brien struggles for answers after final Knights game|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===St Helens R.F.C.===
On 20 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[St Helens RFC]] in the [[Super League]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=20 Nov 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Jackson Hastings makes shock St Helens move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/jackson-hastings-makes-shock-st-helens-move/}}</ref>
On 7 February 2026, Hastings made his club debut for St Helens in their 98-2 victory over [[Workington Town]] in the third round of the Challenge Cup.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/25834740.paul-rowley-said-st-helens-cup-win-workington/|title=What Paul Rowley said about St Helens' cup win at Workington|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}}</ref>

==Representative career==
He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|publisher=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref> He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test début in the defeat by Tonga.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|publisher=Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>

On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] in their 26-24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==Personal life==
Hastings is the son of Megan Harrod and former [[Sydney Roosters]] player [[Kevin Hastings]].<ref name="dailytelegraph3"/><ref name="foxsports">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au//nrl/nrl-rising-star-sydney-roosters-recruit-young-halfback-jackson-hastings/story-e6frf3ou-1226832712123|title=NRL rising star: Sydney Roosters recruit, young halfback Jackson Hastings|publisher=foxsports.com.au|access-date=2014-09-04}}</ref>
